# Dialog

A modal window layered over the page for focused content and actions.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Structure and accessibility](#structure-and-accessibility)
- [Controlled state](#controlled-state)
- [Scrollable content](#scrollable-content)

## Installation

Add the Dialog component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/dialog
```

## Usage

Use Dialog for a short, focused workflow that needs context from the current screen, such as editing a record or reviewing details. Avoid moving a full page or a long multi-step task into a modal.

```vue
<template>
  <Dialog>
    <DialogTrigger as-child>
      <Button variant="outline">Edit profile</Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent class="sm:max-w-md">
      <DialogHeader>
        <DialogTitle>Edit profile</DialogTitle>
        <DialogDescription>Update the public details shown on your account.</DialogDescription>
      </DialogHeader>
      <FieldGroup>
        <Field>
          <FieldLabel for="dialog-name">Name</FieldLabel>
          <Input id="dialog-name" value="Ada Lovelace" />
        </Field>
      </FieldGroup>
      <DialogFooter>
        <DialogClose as-child><Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button></DialogClose>
        <DialogClose as-child><Button>Save changes</Button></DialogClose>
      </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
  </Dialog>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  Dialog,
  DialogClose,
  DialogContent,
  DialogDescription,
  DialogFooter,
  DialogHeader,
  DialogTitle,
  DialogTrigger,
} from '@/Components/Dialog'
import { Field, FieldGroup, FieldLabel } from '@/Components/Field'
import { Input } from '@/Components/Input'
</script>
```

## Structure and accessibility

Every dialog needs a `DialogTitle`. Add `DialogDescription` when the title does not fully explain the task or its consequence. The modal primitive traps focus while open, supports Escape dismissal, and restores focus when it closes. Put explicit actions in `DialogFooter`, and wrap cancel or done controls with `DialogClose` when they should close without changing root state manually.

`DialogTrigger` and `DialogClose` support `as-child`. Use a focusable child and follow the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition) so the primitive's attributes and events reach it.

## Controlled state

Use `default-open` for an initially open uncontrolled dialog, bind `v-model:open` for standard Vue state, or pass a StackTrace `useToggle()` controller to `control`. Controlled state is useful when saving, navigation, or another external event determines when the dialog closes.

## Scrollable content

Use `DialogScrollContent` when content can exceed the viewport. It places the modal in a scrollable overlay so the title and actions remain reachable. If long content is the normal case rather than an exception, prefer a dedicated page.

```vue
<template>
  <Dialog>
    <DialogTrigger as-child>
      <Button variant="outline">Review terms</Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogScrollContent>
      <DialogHeader>
        <DialogTitle>Workspace terms</DialogTitle>
        <DialogDescription>
          Review the important details before accepting.
        </DialogDescription>
      </DialogHeader>
      <div class="space-y-4 text-sm text-muted-foreground">
        <p v-for="section in sections" :key="section.title">
          <strong class="text-foreground">{{ section.title }}.</strong>
          {{ section.content }}
        </p>
      </div>
      <DialogFooter>
        <DialogClose as-child>
          <Button variant="outline">Close</Button>
        </DialogClose>
        <DialogClose as-child>
          <Button>Accept terms</Button>
        </DialogClose>
      </DialogFooter>
    </DialogScrollContent>
  </Dialog>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  Dialog,
  DialogClose,
  DialogDescription,
  DialogFooter,
  DialogHeader,
  DialogScrollContent,
  DialogTitle,
  DialogTrigger,
} from '@/Components/Dialog'

const sections = [
  {
    title: 'Account access',
    content: 'Workspace administrators manage membership and access levels.',
  },
  {
    title: 'Shared content',
    content: 'Items published to the workspace are visible to its members.',
  },
  {
    title: 'Retention',
    content: 'Deleted items remain recoverable for the configured retention period.',
  },
  {
    title: 'Notifications',
    content: 'Important security and membership changes may generate notifications.',
  },
  {
    title: 'Ownership',
    content: 'Workspace owners remain responsible for exporting required records.',
  },
]
</script>
```

> **Use Alert Dialog for consequential decisions: [Alert Dialog](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/alert-dialog)**
>
> Alert Dialog requires an explicit response and better communicates a destructive or otherwise high-consequence decision. Dialog is for focused content and workflows.

Hosted documentation: [Dialog](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/dialog)
